title: Computer Experiment LEarning Curve eXtrapolation
text: Run active learning explore unknown function:
code: library("celecx") library("mlr3") library("mlr3learners")  # for regr.km  # Define objective (unknown function to learn) objective <- ObjectiveRFun$new(   fun = function(xs) list(y = sin(xs$x * pi)),   domain = ps(x = p_dbl(lower = 0, upper = 2)),   codomain = ps(y = p_dbl(tags = "learn")) )  # Run active learning result <- optimize_active(   objective = objective,   n_evals = 10L,   learner = lrn("regr.km", covtype = "matern5_2"),   se_method = "auto",   batch_size = 2L,   acq_evals = 20L,   multipoint_method = "greedy" )  # Access results result$instance$archive$data  # All evaluated points  xvals <- seq(0, 2, length.out = 100) yvals.true <- objective$fun(list(x = xvals))$y surrogate <- result$optimizer$surrogates$uncertainty yvals.pred <- surrogate$predict(data.table::data.table(x = xvals)) plot(xvals, yvals.true, col = "red", type = "l", xlab = "x", ylab = "y",   main = "Active Learning sin(x) with batch_size = 2") lines(xvals, yvals.pred$mean, col = "blue") lines(xvals, with(yvals.pred, mean + 1.96 * se), col = "blue", lty = 2) lines(xvals, with(yvals.pred, mean - 1.96 * se), col = "blue", lty = 2) text(y ~ x, labels = batch_nr, data = result$instance$archive$data, pos = 1)

title: Computer Experiment LEarning Curve eXtrapolation
text: Consider complex 2D test function: example requires kknn package.  use KNN surrogate model, deliberately chosen SE estimation. therefore give se_method = "bootstrap" argument, n_bootstrap = 10 trials (chosen small quick demonstration). propose batch_size = 10 points iteration, top 10 acq_evals = 100 candidate points. can modify candidate set influencing acq_optimizer. current interface, translated candidate sampler acquisition step; use Latin hypercube sampling.
code: objective <- ObjectiveRFun$new(   fun = function(xs) {     bump_a <- exp(-((xs$x1 - 0.3)^2 + (xs$x2 - 0.3)^2) / 0.02)     bump_b <- 0.7 * exp(-((xs$x1 - 0.8)^2 + (xs$x2 - 0.7)^2) / 0.01)     list(y = bump_a + bump_b)   },   domain = ps(x1 = p_dbl(lower = 0, upper = 1), x2 = p_dbl(lower = 0, upper = 1)),   codomain = ps(y = p_dbl(tags = "learn")) )  library("ggplot2") grid <- data.table::CJ(   x1 = seq(0, 1, length.out = 100L),   x2 = seq(0, 1, length.out = 100L) ) grid[, y := objective$fun(list(x1 = x1, x2 = x2))$y]  ggplot(grid, aes(x1, x2, z = y)) +   geom_contour_filled() +   coord_equal() acq_optimizer <- clx_sps("lhs")  result <- optimize_active(   objective = objective,   n_evals = 200L,   learner = lrn("regr.kknn", k = 4L),   se_method = "bootstrap",   n_bootstrap = 10L,   batch_size = 10L,   acq_evals = 100L,   acq_optimizer = acq_optimizer )  result_data <- result$instance$archive$data[, .(x1, x2, y, batch_nr)] result_task <- as_task_regr(result_data[, .(x1, x2, y)], target = "y") kknn_model <- lrn("regr.kknn", k = 3L)$train(result_task)  grid_knn <- data.table::copy(grid) grid_knn[, y := kknn_model$predict_newdata(grid[, .(x1, x2)])$response]  ggplot(grid_knn, aes(x1, x2, z = y)) +   geom_contour_filled() +   geom_point(     data = result_data,     mapping = aes(x1, x2, color = batch_nr),     inherit.aes = FALSE,     shape = 4,     size = 1.5,     stroke = 0.6   ) +   scale_color_gradient(     low = "steelblue",     high = "firebrick",     limits = c(1, 6)   ) +   coord_equal()

title: Active Learning Proposal Context — ALContext
text: Ephemeral context passed OptimizerAL ALProposer objects single outer proposal round. context keeps shallow references optimizer's canonical surrogate acquisition-function registries. Access go $get_surrogate() $get_acq(), methods implement lazy model updates, lazy acquisition-function fitting, proposer-local pending-point filtering. Creates new active-learning context. Adds points selected current proposal batch. Lazily updates returns canonical surrogate. Lazily wires, fits, updates, returns acquisition function. Creates fresh acquisition-function working object. Points evaluated pending evaluation.

title: Gower Active Learning Distance — mlr_al_distances_gower

title: Active Learning Optimizer Factory — optimizer_active_learning
text: learner (mlr3::LearnerRegr) Base regression learner used surrogate. se_method (character(1)) obtain standard errors: "auto": use native "se" supported learner, otherwise "bootstrap". "bootstrap": wrap via LearnerRegrBootstrapSE. "quantile": wrap via LearnerRegrQuantileSE (requires "quantiles" support). n_bootstrap (integer(1)) Number bootstrap replicates "bootstrap". Ignored otherwise. batch_size (integer(1)) Number points proposed per active-learning iteration. multipoint_method (character(1)) Batch selection strategy: "greedy": top-k acquisition score "local_penalization": sequential local-penalization heuristic "diversity": sequential score/diversity trade-"constant_liar": sequential pseudo-label batching acq_optimizer (bbotk::Optimizer | mlr3mbo::AcqOptimizer) Optimizer used choose candidate-generation strategy acquisition scoring. current implementation translates common optimizers SpaceSampler ignores optimizer-specific search logic. acq_evals (integer(1)) Number candidate points scored per proposal round.

title: Convenience Constructor for Pool-Based Active Learning Optimizers — optimizer_pool_al
text: method (character(1)) One "gsx", "gsy", "igs", "qbc", "random", "ideal". learner (mlr3::LearnerRegr | NULL) Regression learner. Required "gsy", "igs", "qbc", "ideal". delta (numeric(1)) Exploration weight IDEAL (default 1). n_init (integer(1) | NULL) Number initial samples. NULL uses OptimizerAL's default initialization policy. init_method (character(1) | NULL) Optional initialization override: "gsx", "random", "kmeans". NULL keeps method-specific default ("kmeans" IDEAL, "gsx" GSx/GSy/iGS, "random" random/QBC). k_qbc (integer(1)) Number QBC committee members (default 5). batch_size (integer(1)) Points per iteration (default 1). pool_size (NULL | integer(1)) Optional number candidate points subsample uniformly scoring. NULL keeps exhaustive pool scoring enable continuous-space use.
